subject: Computer Science
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 5: CYBER SECURITY
topics: Cyber security focuses on keeping digital devices and information safe from threats such as viruses,
hacking, and online scams. In schools, homes, and workplaces in Zambia, people rely on computers and
mobile phones for communication and services. Learning cyber security helps learners protect their data,
avoid cybercrime, and use technology safely and responsibly.
activities: Classroom arranged for small group discussions and board presentation
Tables or desks grouped to allow collaboration
Projector/board to display anonymised cybercrime stories, CIA Triad diagram, and examples
Optional computers for learners to research additional local cyber incidents
assessment: Scenario: Your grandmother receives a text message: “Grandma, it’s me your grandson. I have lost my
phone. This is my new number. Please send me K200 for data so I can call you later.”
(Identifying Threats) What type of cyber threat is this message an example of?
(Policy Development) What are two specific pieces of advice you would give your grandmother to
help her avoid falling for this kind of trick in the future?
